🚦 Exit Strategy Review
🚨 NVDA — STOP HIT
NVIDIA · 1.3799 shares · Entry $217.40 · Stop $204.00 (pre-breakout base)
Current $200.16 — stop breached by $3.84 (1.9% below stop). Price dropped from ~$208.50 (June 20) → $200.04 (June 24) → $200.16 (June 25). Driver: SK Hynix HBM4 production slowdown hit AI chip sector sentiment.

Cost basis: $300.06  |  Net loss: −$26.86 (−9.0% total return)
Market structure: 50-day MA lost ($209–213). Real support at $200.41 (price just below). Next: $196–197. 200-day MA: $189–192.
Stop derivation: Pre-breakout consolidation base — violated. This was a real structural level, not arbitrary.
Pre-market June 26: $201.40 (+0.6%) — minor bounce, still below stop.
Next catalyst: Q2 FY27 earnings August 19, 2026 (55 days) — another binary event ahead.
Re-derived levels: TP1 $238–240 (52wk high $236.54)  |  TP2 $260 ✓  |  TP3 $285 ✓ (analyst avg $306)
Mechanical action: EXIT at market open. The stop was set for this scenario. Holding means operating without a stop through a 55-day wait to the next earnings binary. Your call.
✅ LLY — HOLD
Stop: $915 (well below 200-day MA zone $903–983)  |  Current: ~$1,107  |  TP1 zone: $1,114–$1,125

TP1 trim commission math: 0.0986 × 30% = 0.0296 shares. Gross P&L: ($1,107 − $994.87) × 0.0296 = $3.32  |  Exit commission: −$3.00  |  Net: $0.32 — too thin. HOLD to TP2 $1,243 (analyst median $1,251). Centessa acquisition June 24 expands pipeline. Orforglipron/Foundayo launched. Thesis intact.

Susquehanna raised to $575 (June 22). Bullish golden cross intact. Stop at $430 protects position.
TP1: $460–465  |  TP2: $485  |  TP3: $550–575 (Susquehanna)

No additional small-cap <$2B names with confirmed July events found. CAPR presented as forward-watch.
💵 Cash Deployment — $213.81 Available
After $3 reserve + $3 buy commission: $207.81 deployable. 30% portfolio cap: max ~$246 per catalyst position.
Option A — MNKD catalyst play (July 26)
With current cash: 30 shares × $6.50 = $195 + $3 commission = $198 total. Remaining cash: $15.81 (above $3 reserve ✓). Position: 24% of portfolio.
If NVDA exits first: $273.20 net proceeds → total cash $487 → buy ~36 shares MNKD × $6.50 = $237 total. Cash remaining: $250. Better sizing, more cushion.
Option B — Add to TSM (MODERATE 33/100)
0.47 shares TSM × $441.35 = $207.43 + $3 = $210.43. Blended avg entry: $424.26. Cash remaining: $3.38 ✓. TSM heading to TP2 $485, Susquehanna $575.
⚡ Recommended: WAIT
NVDA below stop, AVGO 2.7% from its stop — deploying now adds risk to a stressed book. Hold $213.81 as dry powder. If NVDA exits → $487 cash → rotate into MNKD. If AVGO also exits → $400+ cash → rebuild deliberately. Your call.
🔄 Rotation Suggestions
NVDA → MNKD (stop-triggered rotation)
NVDA: HIGH risk (58/100), stop hit, SK Hynix sector headwind, Aug 19 binary looming. MNKD: halal ✅, sNDA catalyst July 26 (31 days), existing approval de-risks, revenue growing 64% YoY.

Sell NVDA: 1.3799 shares at ~$200.16  |  Gross: $276.20  |  −$3 commission  |  Net: $273.20  |  Net loss: −$26.86
Buy MNKD: ~36 shares × $6.50 = $237 (incl. $3 commission)  |  Cash remaining after rotation: ~$249

Rationale: Cuts the stop-hit position, removes Aug 19 earnings binary, redeploys into a near-term halal catalyst with lower structural risk.
Risk: MNKD sNDA rejection (~15%); NVDA pre-market $201.40 shows marginal bounce.
Your call.
TSM — No trim yet, hold to TP2
Commission math at $441 too thin ($2.94 net). Wait for revised TP1 $460–465 or TP2 $485. Re-evaluate at $460.
📚 Today's Concept — Day 27 of 30
FCF Part 2 — Owner Earnings
Standard FCF = operating cash flow minus total capex. Buffett's refinement — owner earnings — strips it further: net income + D&A minus maintenance capex only. Growth capex is discretionary; money you spend to capture future markets doesn't count. For fabless names like NVDA and AVGO, maintenance capex is essentially zero — no fabs to maintain. Their owner earnings ≈ operating cash flow, which is far above GAAP net income after stripping ~$1.8B/quarter in stock-based comp (real cost, but zero cash). This is why institutional models price these names on adjusted FCF yield, not P/E. When someone says NVDA is expensive at 35x earnings, they're using the wrong denominator — adj FCF is the number that moves price targets.
Also today:
Capex intensity ratio (capex ÷ revenue): NVDA ~1–2% (fabless, asset-light). TSM ~35% (foundry, capital-intensive). Same supply chain, completely different valuation frameworks — this ratio is a core reason why analysts structurally discount TSM vs. NVDA despite TSM physically manufacturing the chips NVDA designs.
